source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A regular octahedron has eight equilateral triangle faces with four faces meeting at each vertex. Jun will make the regular octahedron shown on the right by folding the piece of paper shown on the left. Which numbered face will end up to the right of $Q$?

<image1>
extraction_notes: No explicit choice mapping is present in the record, so the numeric answer is kept as-is.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says Jun will fold 'the piece of paper shown', so the net diagram is required
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is primarily visual rather than solvable from text alone
the target relation 'to the right of Q' depends on face placement/orientation in the shown figure
multi_step: solving requires reconstructing a regular octahedron from a 2D net and tracking which numbered face maps next to face Q after folding
the prompt asks for a directional spatial relation ('to the right of Q'), which adds an orientation step beyond simple adjacency
the task is not a direct lookup; it combines net interpretation with 3D spatial reasoning
verifiability: answer_text is a single numeric label ('1'), so final-answer checking is straightforward
answer_type is numeric/number and answer_present=true, supporting deterministic evaluation
registry_passed=true and the image asset exists with high readability_score=0.8974, which supports downstream verification from the provided diagram

decision_reasons: assets are complete: question, image, and numeric answer are all present with no registry issues
initial scores indicate strong image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and good verifiability
risk flag 'spatial-orientation-ambiguity' suggests the sample may need follow-up review before entering the higher-priority pool
core semantics are recoverable, so it should not be rejected at collection stage
